contributor: university of illinois at urbana-champaign library, urbana, il
description: GREENVILLE ADVOCATE J F ALEXANDER Editor TlllRSDil OC T 1ft 1858 Jehu Baker Esq Ou Saturday last Mr Baker deliver ed a very powerful and convincing speech to the citizens of this place on the principles which control the differ ent political parlies of the present day lie is an original man and makes on original speech His speech at this place was entirely different from any thing ever delivered here before lie is a man of extraordinary talent and is a very eloquent and effective speaker His positions are impregnable and his arguments incontrovertible But as we publish this week an abridgement of bis Alton speech which is somewhat similar to the one delivered here and as all who did not hear him will have an opportunity of reading it we forbear further comment In regard to Mr Bakers chances for election we must say they are very flit tering In this county the Old Whig and Fillmore vote is united almost to a man in his favor while all the Republi cans and mary Democrats will vote for him in preference to Fouke or Hope His majority in this county cannot be less than three hundred In Madison
